title: I Signed the Lease on My First Apartment
excerpt: My hands were shaking when I signed my name, and not just because of the rent.
contentHtml:
My hands were shaking when I signed my name, and not just because of the rent. There is something surreal about a stack of paper turning into a set of keys — into a door that is, for the first time, entirely mine to lock and unlock.

Nobody warns you how many feelings fit into one afternoon: pride, panic, and a strange kind of grief for the version of me who is still figuring out how to be a grown-up. I stood in the empty living room and just breathed for a minute.

Growing up doesn't happen in one big leap. It happens in a hundred small signatures.

Tonight I'll sleep on a mattress on the floor, surrounded by boxes, and honestly? I can't stop smiling.

title: This Year I Want to Stop Apologizing for Taking Up Space
excerpt: A small list of things I'm trying to unlearn this year, one awkward sentence at a time.
contentHtml:
I counted once: I said "sorry" fourteen times in a single meeting, for things that needed no apology at all. This year that's the habit I want to unlearn.

  • Saying what I actually think, the first time, without a disclaimer
  • Letting silence sit instead of filling it with excuses
  • Taking the seat at the table instead of hovering near the door

None of this is dramatic. It's quiet, everyday work — but it's mine, and I'm doing it on purpose.

title: Nobody Tells You How Loud Silence Can Get in Your Late Twenties
excerpt: Somewhere between 25 and 30, the group chats slow down and the Sunday phone calls get quieter.
contentHtml:
Somewhere between twenty-five and thirty, the group chats slow down. People move cities, get married, change time zones, and the Sunday phone calls get quieter without anyone deciding it that way.

I used to think that silence meant something was wrong. Now I think it just means everyone is busy building their own version of a life, carefully, in their own room.

Maybe the goal was never to keep every room full. Maybe it's to like the person standing in mine.
